title: Word problems with volume
description: Solve word problems involving volume.  Estimate the volume of items.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ayumi puts $3$ packets of ketchup on her tater tots.  The volume of each packet of ketchup is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

  
**How many milliliters of ketchup does Ayumi put on her tater tots?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
One packet of ketchup has a volume of $\maroonD8$ milliliters.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of $\text{mL}$ of ketchup by multiplying the number of $\text{mL}$ in each packet  $(\maroonD{8 \text { mL}})$ by the number of packets $(\greenD {{3} \text { packets}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{8}\text{ mL} \times \greenD{3}=\purpleD{24}\text{ mL}$
Ayumi puts $\purpleD{24 \text { mL}}$ of ketchup on her tater tots.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder with tick marks labeled at 5 milliliters and 10 milliliters. 4 unlabeled tick marks are shown before 5 milliliters and 4 unlabeled tick marks are shown before 10 milliliters. The ketchup inside the graduated cylinder measures to the third tick mark after 5 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Walter Wizard is making $7$ magic potions.  Each potion will have the same amount of liquid.  One potion is shown below.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many total liters of potion is Walter Wizard making?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters
hints:
One potion is $\purpleD{4}$ liters.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the total number of liters of potion by multiplying the number of liters in each potion  $(\purpleD{4 \text { liters}})$ by the number of potions $(\blueD{{7} \text { potions}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{4}\times\blueD{7}\text{ L} =\greenD{28}\text{ L}$
Walter Wizard is making a total of $\greenD{28 \text { liters}}$ of potion.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder with tick marks labeled at 5 liters and 10 liters. 4 unlabeled tick marks are shown below 5 liters and 4 unlabeled tick marks are shown below 10 liters. The potion inside the graduated cylinder measures to the fourth tick mark from the bottom of the cylinder, 1 tick mark below 5 liters mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
Soup Shack sold $5$ servings of tomato soup at lunch on Tuesday.  One serving is shown below.  Each serving of tomato soup had the same number of fluid ounces.
  
[[☃ image 1]]

**How many fluid ounces of tomato soup did Soup Shack sell at lunch on Tuesday?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] fluid ounces
hints:
One serving of soup is $\redD{11}$ fluid ounces.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the number of fluid ounces Soup Shack sold at lunch by multiplying the number of fluid ounces in each serving  $(\redD{11 \text { fluid ounces}})$ by the number of servings sold $(\blueD{{5} \text { servings}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$ \redD{11}\text{ fl oz}\times \blueD{5}=\greenD{55}\text{ fl oz}$
Soup Shack sold $\greenD{55 \text { fluid ounces}}$ of tomato soup at lunch on Tuesday.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 2 fluid ounces to 18 fluid ounces. Tick marks 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, and 18 fluid ounces are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 2 fluid ounces, 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown after 18 fluid ounces, and there is 1 unlabeled tick mark between every 2 labeled tick marks. The tomato soup inside the graduated cylinder measures to the unlabeled tick mark above 10 fluid ounces.

assessment_items.question_content:
Percy Pig uses a hose to wash his pig sty for $11$ minutes.  The hose uses $7$ liters of water every minute.
  
**How many liters of water did Percy Pig use to wash his pig sty?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters
hints:
We can find the total number of liters of water by multiplying the number of liters in each minute  $(\purpleD{7 \text { liters}})$ by the number of minutes $(\blueD{{11} \text { minutes}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$ \purpleD{7}\text{ L}\times \blueD{11}=\greenD{77 }\text{ L}$

Percy Pig used $\greenD{77 \text { liters}}$ of water to wash his pig sty.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kirsten has $9$ syrup containers from a local cafe. There are $6$ milliliters of syrup per container.
  
**How many milliliters of syrup does Kirsten have?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
We can find the total number of milliliters of syrup by multiplying the number of containers $(\purpleD{9})$ by the number of milliliters in each container $(\blueD{{6} \text { mL}})$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$ \purpleD{9}\times \blueD{6}\text{ mL}=\greenD{54 }\text{ mL}$

Kirsten has $\greenD{54 \text { milliliters}}$ of syrup.

assessment_items.question_content:
Monty Mouse has some friends over.  He shares all of his juice (shown below) with his friends. He gives each friend $5$ milliliters of juice.

 

[[☃ image 1]]

  **How many friends does Monty have over?**  
*Monty does not drink any juice himself.*


[[☃ input-number 1]] friends
hints:
The juice container is filled to $\goldE{{60} \text { mL}}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the number of friends by dividing the total amount of juice in the container $(\goldE{{60} \text { mL}})$ by the number of milliliters he gives each friend $(\blueD{5 \text { mL}})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\goldE{60}\text{ mL}\div\blueD{5}\text{ mL}=\greenD{12}$
Monty Mouse has $\green{12 \text {}}$ friends over.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 10 milliliters to 100 milliliters. Tick marks 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, and 100 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 10 milliliters and 1 unlabeled tick mark is between every 2 labeled tick marks. The juice inside the graduated cylinder measures to the tick mark 60 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
John's stomach feels sick!  The doctor gives John some medicine (shown below) and tells him to take $4$ milliliters every day until the medicine is finished.

[[☃ image 1]]

  
  **How many days will it take John to finish the medicine?**   

[[☃ input-number 1]] days
hints:
The container is filled to $\maroonD{40} \text { mL}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the number of days it will take John to finish the medicine by dividing the total amount of medicine in the container $(\maroonD{40} \text { mL})$ by the number of $\text{mL}$ in each dose $(\blueD{4} \text { mL})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{40}\text{ mL}\div\blueD{4}\text{ mL}=\greenD{10}\text{ days}$
It will take John $\greenD{10 \text { days}}$ to finish the medicine.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 10 milliliters to 70 milliliters. Tick marks 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, and 70 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 10 milliliters and 1 unlabeled tick mark is between every 2 labeled tick marks. The medicine inside the graduated cylinder measures to the tick mark 40 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sarah put $5$ packets of honey into the container below.  All of the packets held the same amount of honey.

[[☃ image 1]]

  
  **How many milliliters of honey were in each packet?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
The container is filled to $\goldE{45} \text { mL}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The number of $\text{mL}$ of honey  in each packet is the total number of $\text{mL}$  in the container $(\goldE{45} \text { mL})$ divided by the number of packets $(\blueD5)$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{45}\text{ mL} \div \blueD{5}=\greenD9\text{ mL}$
There were $\greenD{9 \text { mL}}$ of honey in each packet.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 10 milliliters to 70 milliliters. Tick marks 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, and 70 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 10 milliliters and there is 1 unlabeled tick mark between every 2 labeled tick marks. The honey inside the graduated cylinder measures to the unlabeled tick mark above 40 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ryan watered his plants for $8$ minutes using a sprinkler.  The sprinkler used a total of $72$ liters of water.  
  
**How many liters of water did the sprinkler use each minute?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters per minute
hints:
We can find the number of liters per minute by dividing the total amount of water $(\maroonD{72} \text { liters})$ by the number of minutes Ryan watered his plants $(\blueD{8} \text { minutes})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{72}\text{ L}\div\blueD{8}=\greenD{9}\text{ L}$
Ryan's sprinkler used $\greenD{9 \text { liters}}$ of water each minute.

assessment_items.question_content:
Maricel drank her entire $50$ milliliter sports drink during her $10$ kilometer race.  Every kilometer Maricel drank the same amount.  
  
**How many milliliters did Maricel drink each kilometer?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
We can find the number of $\text {mL}$ Maricel drank each kilometer by dividing the total number of $\text {mL}$ $(\maroonD{50} \text { mL})$ by the number of kilometers she ran $(\blueD{10} \text { km})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{50}\text{ mL}\div \blueD{10}\text{ km}=\greenD{5}\text{ mL}$
Maricel drank $\greenD{5 \text { mL}}$ of her sports drink every kilometer.

assessment_items.question_content:
Soapy Suds laundry detergent comes in a $21 \text{}$ fluid ounce bottle.  Each load of laundry with Soapy Suds uses $3$ fluid ounces of detergent.  
  
**How many loads of laundry can be washed with a bottle of Soapy Suds laundry detergent?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] loads
hints:
We can find the number of laundry loads by dividing the total amount of detergent $(\maroonD{21} \text { fluid ounces})$ by the number of fluid ounces needed for each load $(\blueD{3} \text { fluid ounces})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{21}\text{ fl oz}\div\blueD{3}\text{ fl oz}=\greenD{7}\text{ loads}$
$\greenD{7 \text {}}$ loads of laundry can be washed with the Soapy Suds laundry detergent bottle.

assessment_items.question_content:
Bert the bunny is going to use up all of the shampoo and conditioner shown below to wash his fur today.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How many milliliters of shampoo and conditioner does Bert use to wash his fur?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
Bert uses $\goldE{14\text { mL}}$ of shampoo.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Bert uses $\greenD{12\text { mL}}$ of conditioner.


[[☃ image 1]]

We add the measurements together to find how many milliliters of shampoo $(\goldE{14 \text { mL}})$ and conditioner $(\greenD{12 \text { mL}})$ Bert uses.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE {14} \text { mL}  + \greenD{12} \text { mL}  =   \blueD{26}\text { mL}$ 
Bert uses $\blueD{26\text { mL}}$ of shampoo and conditioner to wash his hair.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 3 milliliters to 18 milliliters. Tick marks 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, and 18 milliliters are labeled. 2 unlabeled tick marks are shown before 3 milliliters, 2 unlabeled tick marks are shown after 18 milliliters, and there are 2 unlabeled tick marks between every 2 labeled tick marks. The shampoo inside the graduated cylinder measures to the second unlabeled tick mark above 12 milliliters.

A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 3 milliliters to 18 milliliters. Tick marks 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, and 18 milliliters are labeled. 2 unlabeled tick marks are shown before 3 milliliters, 2 unlabeled tick marks are shown after 18 milliliters, and there are 2 unlabeled tick marks between every 2 labeled tick marks. The conditioner inside the graduated cylinder measures to the tick mark 12 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Benardo is making fruit punch by combining all of the orange and grape juice shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How many milliliters of fruit punch will he make?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
There are $\goldE{200}\text { mL}$ of orange juice.


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\purpleD{250}\text { mL}$ of grape juice.  

[[☃ image 1]]

We add the measurements together to find how many milliliters of fruit juice he will combine.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\goldE{200} \text { mL} + \purpleD{250} \text { mL}   =   \greenD{450}\text { mL}$ 
He will make $\greenD{450}\text { mL}$ of fruit punch.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 50 milliliters to 300 milliliters. Tick marks 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, and 300 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 50 milliliters and 1 unlabeled tick mark is between every 2 labeled tick marks. The orange juice inside the graduated cylinder measures to the tick mark 200 milliliters.

A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 50 milliliters to 300 milliliters. Tick marks 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, and 300 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 50 milliliters and 1 unlabeled tick mark is between every 2 labeled tick marks. The grape juice inside the graduated cylinder measures to the tick mark 250 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Li Min squeezed $2$ lemons to make lemonade.  She squeezed $42$ milliliters of lemon juice from the first lemon and $36$ milliliters of juice from the second lemon.

**How much lemon juice did Li Min squeeze from her $2$ lemons?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
To find the total amount of lemon juice, we add the number of $\text{mL}$ she squeezed from the first lemon $(\blueD{42} \text { mL})$ and the second lemon $(\maroonD{36} \text { mL})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{42}\text { mL} +  \maroonD{36} \text { mL}=   \greenD{78}\text { mL}$
Li Min squeezed $\greenD{78}\text { mL}$ of lemon juice from her $2$ lemons.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ella's mom pours $205$ milliliters of coffee into a mug.  Then she adds $25$ milliliters of milk to her coffee.   

**How many milliliters of liquid are in her mug now?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
To find the total number of $\text{mL}$, we add the number of $\text{mL}$ of coffee $(\blueD{205} \text { mL})$ to the number of $\text{mL}$ of milk $(\maroonD{25} \text { mL})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{205}\text { mL} + \maroonD{25} \text { mL}  =   \greenD{230}\text { mL}$
There are $\greenD{230}\text { mL}$ of liquid in Ella's mom's mug.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kathiva has a baby pool in her backyard.  Yesterday, the pool had $425$ liters of water in it.  This afternoon, Kathiva used a hose to add $23$ more liters of water.  

**How many liters of water are in the baby pool now?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters
hints:
To find the number of liters in the pool now, we add the number of liters yesterday $(\blueD{425} \text { L})$ and the number of liters from the hose $(\purpleD{23} \text { L})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{425}\text { L} + \purpleD{23} \text { L}  =   \greenD{448}\text { L}$
There are $\greenD{448}\text { L}$ of water in the baby pool now.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ahmed made a fruit smoothie in his blender, shown below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

He gave $320$ milliliters of the smoothie to his brother.   

**How much smoothie is left for Ahmed to drink?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
Ahmed made $\maroonD{625}$ milliliters of fruit smoothie in his blender. 

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the amount of smoothie left for Ahmed, we subtract the number of $\text{mL}$ he gave his brother $(\blueD{320 \text { mL}})$  from the total number of $\text{mL}$ he made $(\maroonD{625 \text { mL}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{625} \text { mL} - \blueD{320} \text { mL}    =   \greenD{305}\text { mL}$
Ahmed has $\greenD{305\text { mL}}$  of fruit smoothie left to drink.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 100 milliliters to 700 milliliters. Tick marks 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, and 700 milliliters are labeled. 3 unlabeled tick marks are shown before 100 milliliters and there are 3 unlabeled tick marks between every 2 labeled tick marks. The smoothie inside the graduated cylinder measures to the first unlabeled tick mark above 600 milliliters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chioma loves yellow!  She buys a container with the amount of yellow paint shown below.  She uses $14$ liters of paint to paint her walls.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How much yellow paint does Chioma have left?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] liters
hints:
There are $\goldE{25\text { L}}$ of yellow paint.

[[☃ image 1]]

To find the amount of paint Chioma has left, we subtract the number of $\text{L}$ used to paint walls $(\blueD{14} \text { L})$ from the number of liters Chioma started with$(\goldE{25} \text { L})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\goldE{25} \text { L} - \blueD{14} \text { L}  =   \greenD{11}\text { L}$
Chioma has $\greenD{11}\text { L}$ of yellow paint left.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder with tick marks from 5 liters to 30 liters. Tick marks 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, and 30 liters are labeled. The yellow paint inside the graduated cylinder measures to the 25 liters tick mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elina has a glass of chocolate milk (shown below). She made it by mixing $237$ milliliters of milk with some chocolate syrup.  


[[☃ image 1]]

**How much chocolate syrup did Elina use to make her glass of chocolate milk?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
The glass of chocolate milk is filled to $\goldE{275} \text{ mL}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

To find the amount of chocolate syrup Elina used, we subtract the number of $\text{mL}$ of milk $(\blueD{237} \text { mL})$ from the total number of $\text{mL}$ she has now $(\goldE {275}\text { mL})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\goldE{275}\text { mL} - \blueD{237} \text { mL}  =   \greenD{38}\text { mL}$
Elina used $\greenD{38}\text { mL}$ of chocolate syrup to make her glass of chocolate milk.

options.alt: A graduated cylinder labeled with tick marks from 50 milliliters to 300 milliliters. Tick marks 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, and 300 milliliters are labeled. 1 unlabeled tick mark is shown before 50 milliliters and 1 unlabeled tick mark is between every 2 labeled tick marks. The chocolate milk inside the graduated cylinder measures to the unlabeled tick mark above the 250 milliliters tick mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
Henry's liquid soap container was partially full.  Then he added $177 $ milliliters more soap to the container.  Now, the container has a total of $266$ milliliters of soap.

**How much soap was in the container before Henry added more?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
To find the amount of soap Henry started with, we subtract the number of $\text{mL}$ Henry added $(\blueD{177} \text { mL})$  from the number of $\text{mL}$ he has now $(\maroonD{266} \text { mL})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{266} \text { mL} - \blueD{177} \text { mL} =   \greenD{89}\text { mL}$
The soap container had $\greenD{89}\text { mL}$ of soap before Henry added more.

assessment_items.question_content:
Matt bought a $650$ milliliter container of cooking oil. He used  $136$ milliliters of the oil making waffles.

**How much cooking oil does Matt have left?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] milliliters
hints:
To find the amount of oil left, we subtract the number of $\text{mL}$ Matt used to make waffles $(\blueD{136 \text { mL}})$  from the total number of $\text{mL}$ he had to start $(\maroonD{650\text { mL}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{650}\text { mL} - \blueD{136} \text { mL} =   \greenD{514}\text { mL}$
Matt has $\greenD{514}\text { mL}$ of cooking oil left.
